title: sium neurophyllum
intro: Sium neurophyllum, also known as water parsnip, is a perennial aquatic plant that grows in shallow water and along riverbanks. Its roots and rhizomes are traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with Sium neurophyllum include its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Sium neurophyllum is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction, and can also be used in salves and creams for topical application.
